title: Neuro-Symbolic Approaches to Compositional Generalization
summary: This thesis explores how combining neural networks with symbolic logic engines can significantly improve an AI system's ability to generalize compositionally. It details an architecture where a neural parser maps natural language into a DSL, and a symbolic engine evaluates the DSL.

key_claims.claim: Constraining neural generation using an EBNF grammar reduces syntactically invalid DSL generations by 98%.
Bayesian updating of grammar weights based on successful traces enables zero-shot domain adaptation.

methods.name: Bayesian EBNF Weighting
methods.description: A method to dynamically adjust the probabilities of grammar rules based on the success/failure of past parses.

limitations: The Bayesian updating mechanism assumes a relatively stable underlying grammar structure.
Evaluation was primarily on synthetic datasets (SCAN).
applicability_to_tars: High. The Bayesian EBNF weighting perfectly validates the current approach in Tars.Evolution where probabilistic grammars are updated.
applicability_to_ix: Medium. The symbolic evaluator component could potentially map to IX skill execution.
applicability_to_ga: Medium. Could improve music-theory hypothesis translation.

suggested_followups.title: Cross-reference thesis Bayesian EBNF weights with Tars.Evolution implementation
suggested_followups.description: Review Chapter 5 of the thesis to see if their weight-update formula can improve our PromotionIndex logic.

provenance: Ingested via tech-watch semantic sweep run #842.
